en_definition: to have no more of something left
example1: We ran out of milk this morning.
example1_ru: У нас закончилось молоко этим утром.
example2: She ran out of time before finishing the test.

en_definition: to prepare or arrange something so it is ready to use
example1: It took an hour to set up the new printer.
example1_ru: Понадобился час, чтобы настроить новый принтер.
example2: We set up the tent before it got dark.

en_definition: to assemble parts to make something whole
example1: He put together the new desk in twenty minutes.
example1_ru: Он собрал новый стол за двадцать минут.
example2: We put together a short presentation for the class.

en_definition: to separate something into its individual pieces
example1: He took apart the old radio to see how it worked.
example1_ru: Он разобрал старое радио, чтобы посмотреть, как оно работает.
example2: The mechanic took apart the engine for repair.

en_definition: to give something to someone without asking for payment
example1: She gave away her old textbooks to a junior student.
example1_ru: Она отдала свои старые учебники студенту младшего курса.
example2: We gave away extra food after the event.

en_definition: to get rid of something you no longer want
example1: Don't throw away that receipt; you might need it.
example1_ru: Не выбрасывай этот чек, он может понадобиться.
example2: He threw away the broken umbrella.

en_definition: to choose something carefully from a group
example1: She picked out a nice gift for her host family.
example1_ru: Она выбрала хороший подарок для своей принимающей семьи.
example2: Pick out the sentence that best describes the picture.

en_definition: to give something to another person, often formally
example1: Please hand over your ID before entering the exam room.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, передайте свой студенческий билет перед входом в аудиторию.
example2: He handed over the keys to the new tenant.

en_definition: to examine or investigate a matter carefully
example1: The office promised to look into the billing error.
example1_ru: Деканат пообещал разобраться с ошибкой в счёте.
example2: I will look into cheaper flight options.

en_definition: to find or meet something by chance
example1: I came across an old photo while cleaning my room.
example1_ru: Я случайно наткнулся на старую фотографию, убирая свою комнату.
example2: She came across a useful website for grammar practice.

en_definition: to continue doing something
example1: Please carry on with your work; I'll wait outside.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, продолжайте свою работу, я подожду снаружи.
example2: They carried on talking after the bell rang.

en_definition: used to give someone permission to do something
example1: Go ahead and ask your question now.
example1_ru: Давай, задавай свой вопрос сейчас.
example2: She said we could go ahead without her.

en_definition: to do something one after another, not at the same time
example1: Let's take turns using the laptop for the project.
example1_ru: Давай делать по очереди, используя ноутбук для проекта.
example2: The children took turns on the swing.

en_definition: to stay somewhere until a person or event arrives
example1: We waited for the bus in the cold for ages.
example1_ru: Мы долго ждали автобус на холоде.
example2: She waited for her friend outside the library.

en_definition: to move or act more quickly
example1: Hurry up, or we'll miss the train!
example1_ru: Поторопись, а то мы опоздаем на поезд!
example2: He hurried up to catch the closing doors.

en_definition: to arrive at a place, especially an event
example1: She showed up late to the group meeting.
example1_ru: Она пришла с опозданием на групповую встречу.
example2: Only half the class showed up for the review session.

en_definition: to wait your turn in a row of people
example1: We stood in line for nearly an hour.
example1_ru: Мы стояли в очереди почти час.
example2: Students stood in line to collect their ID cards.

en_definition: to meet someone unexpectedly
example1: I ran into my old classmate at the mall.
example1_ru: Я случайно встретил своего старого одноклассника в торговом центре.
example2: We ran into some trouble finding the address.

en_definition: to delay doing something until later
example1: Don't put off your homework until the last minute.
example1_ru: Не откладывай домашнее задание на последнюю минуту.
example2: She put off her doctor's appointment twice.

en_definition: to start to understand something
example1: It took me a while to catch on to the new rules.
example1_ru: Мне потребовалось время, чтобы уловить суть новых правил.
example2: He quickly caught on to the game.

en_definition: to distribute something, or to faint
example1: The teacher passed out the test papers.
example1_ru: Учитель раздал экзаменационные листы.
example2: She almost passed out from the heat.

en_definition: to stop working because of a fault
example1: The washing machine broke down again last night.
example1_ru: Стиральная машина снова сломалась прошлой ночью.
example2: Our car broke down on the way to the airport.

en_definition: to think of an idea or plan
example1: She came up with a great idea for the project.
example1_ru: Она придумала отличную идею для проекта.
example2: We need to come up with a backup plan quickly.

en_definition: to continue following a plan or rule without changing it
example1: Try to stick to your study schedule this week.
example1_ru: Постарайся придерживаться своего расписания учёбы на этой неделе.
example2: He stuck to his diet even during the holidays.

en_definition: to make sure you know the current state of something
example1: I use an app to keep track of my expenses.
example1_ru: Я использую приложение, чтобы отслеживать свои расходы.
example2: It's hard to keep track of all the deadlines.

en_definition: to successfully deal with a difficult situation
example1: We finally got through the busy exam week.
example1_ru: Мы наконец пережили напряжённую экзаменационную неделю.
example2: She got through the interview without any problems.

en_definition: to make people feel more relaxed at the start of a meeting
example1: The teacher told a joke to break the ice.
example1_ru: Учитель рассказал шутку, чтобы растопить лёд.
example2: A simple question can break the ice with strangers.

en_definition: to become friendly with new people
example1: It's easy to make friends during orientation week.
example1_ru: Легко заводить друзей во время ознакомительной недели.
example2: She made friends quickly in her new dormitory.

en_definition: to spend time relaxing with someone
example1: We usually hang out with friends after class.
example1_ru: Мы обычно проводим время с друзьями после занятий.
example2: He likes to hang out with his roommates on weekends.

en_definition: to have a friendly relationship with someone
example1: I get along with my roommate very well.
example1_ru: Я хорошо лажу со своим соседом по комнате.
example2: She gets along with almost everyone in class.

en_definition: to fail to keep up with the required pace of work
example1: Don't fall behind on your reading assignments.
example1_ru: Не отставай от своих заданий по чтению.
example2: He fell behind after missing two weeks of class.

en_definition: to do work you missed in order to reach the same level as others
example1: I need to catch up on the lectures I missed.
example1_ru: Мне нужно наверстать лекции, которые я пропустил.
example2: She spent the weekend catching up on homework.

en_definition: to continue paying close attention to a task
example1: It's hard to stay focused during long lectures.
example1_ru: Трудно оставаться сосредоточенным на длинных лекциях.
example2: He stayed focused despite the noise outside.

en_definition: to listen or watch carefully
example1: Please pay attention during the safety instructions.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, обращайте внимание во время инструктажа по безопасности.
example2: She always pays attention in chemistry class.

en_definition: to think about pleasant things instead of paying attention
example1: He daydreamed about summer during the boring lecture.
example1_ru: Он мечтал о лете во время скучной лекции.
example2: Try not to daydream during the exam.

en_definition: to stop working for a short time to rest
example1: Let's take a break after this chapter.
example1_ru: Давай сделаем перерыв после этой главы.
example2: We took a break between the two exams.

en_definition: to delay doing something you should be doing
example1: I tend to procrastinate before big deadlines.
example1_ru: Я склонен откладывать дела перед большими сроками сдачи.
example2: Stop procrastinating and start your essay today.

en_definition: to arrange your tasks and things in a clear, ordered way
example1: You need to get organized before the busy exam period.
example1_ru: Тебе нужно организоваться перед напряжённым периодом экзаменов.
example2: A planner helps me get organized every week.

en_definition: a notebook or app used to organize tasks and dates
example1: I write all my deadlines in my planner.
example1_ru: Я записываю все свои сроки сдачи в свой планировщик.
example2: Her planner is full of colorful sticky notes.

en_definition: a small piece of paper with glue on the back, used for reminders
example1: I left a sticky note on the fridge with a reminder.
example1_ru: Я оставил стикер на холодильнике с напоминанием.
example2: Sticky notes covered his entire desk before exams.

en_definition: to mark important text with a bright color
example1: Highlight the key words in each paragraph.
example1_ru: Выдели ключевые слова в каждом абзаце.
example2: She highlighted the dates she needed to remember.

en_definition: a short line of text marked with a dot in a list
example1: List the main ideas as bullet points.
example1_ru: Перечисли основные идеи в виде маркированных пунктов.
example2: Each slide had three bullet points at most.

en_definition: to describe the basic structure of a plan
example1: The teacher asked us to outline a plan for the essay.
example1_ru: Учитель попросил нас наметить план для эссе.
example2: He outlined a plan before starting the project.

en_definition: an early, unfinished version of a piece of writing
example1: This is just a draft version of my report.
example1_ru: Это всего лишь черновой вариант моего отчёта.
example2: The draft version had several spelling mistakes.

en_definition: the completed and polished version of a document
example1: Send me the final version by tomorrow evening.
example1_ru: Отправь мне финальную версию завтра вечером.
example2: The final version looked much better than the first draft.

en_definition: the total number of words in a piece of writing
example1: The essay's word count must be under one thousand.
example1_ru: Количество слов в эссе должно быть меньше тысячи.
example2: Check the word count before submitting your essay.

en_definition: a list of sources used in a piece of academic writing
example1: Add a bibliography at the end of your essay.
example1_ru: Добавь библиографию в конце своего эссе.
example2: The bibliography listed ten different books.

en_definition: a book, website, or person that provides information
example1: Always name your source when quoting information.
example1_ru: Всегда указывай источник, когда цитируешь информацию.
example2: The article used three reliable sources.

en_definition: to copy someone's exact words in your own writing
example1: You should quote directly when using an author's exact words.
example1_ru: Тебе следует цитировать дословно, когда используешь точные слова автора.
example2: She quoted directly from the textbook in her essay.

en_definition: using your own way of expressing an idea, not copying
example1: Please explain the theory in your own words.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, объясни теорию своими словами.
example2: He answered the question in his own words.

en_definition: the most important point in a text or paragraph
example1: What is the main idea of this paragraph?
example1_ru: Какова основная мысль этого абзаца?
example2: She underlined the main idea in each section.

en_definition: extra information that explains or proves the main idea
example1: Each paragraph should include one supporting detail.
example1_ru: Каждый абзац должен содержать одну вспомогательную деталь.
example2: The essay lacked enough supporting details.

en_definition: to read something quickly to get the general idea
example1: I skimmed the article before reading it carefully.
example1_ru: Я бегло просмотрел статью перед внимательным чтением.
example2: Skim the chapter first, then read it in detail.

en_definition: to look through a text quickly to find specific facts
example1: Scan for information about dates and numbers.
example1_ru: Ищи в тексте информацию о датах и цифрах.
example2: She scanned for information before writing her notes.

en_definition: the ability to understand something you read or hear
example1: The test checks your reading comprehension skills.
example1_ru: Тест проверяет твои навыки понимания прочитанного.
example2: His listening comprehension improved a lot this term.

en_definition: information around a word that helps guess its meaning
example1: Use context clues to guess the meaning of new words.
example1_ru: Используй контекстные подсказки, чтобы угадать значение новых слов.
example2: The sentence gave a clear context clue.

en_definition: a word that has almost the same meaning as another word
example1: Happy is a synonym for glad.
example1_ru: Happy — синоним слова glad.
example2: Find a synonym for the underlined word.

en_definition: a word that means the opposite of another word
example1: Cold is the antonym of hot.
example1_ru: Cold — антоним слова hot.
example2: Match each word with its antonym.

en_definition: the correct order of letters in a word
example1: Check your spelling before submitting the essay.
example1_ru: Проверь своё правописание перед сдачей эссе.
example2: His spelling has improved a lot this year.

en_definition: the marks like commas and periods used in writing
example1: Poor punctuation can make a sentence confusing.
example1_ru: Плохая пунктуация может сделать предложение непонятным.
example2: She fixed the punctuation errors in her essay.

en_definition: an uppercase letter used at the start of sentences and names
example1: Start every sentence with a capital letter.
example1_ru: Начинай каждое предложение с заглавной буквы.
example2: Names always begin with a capital letter.

en_definition: the punctuation mark used at the end of a sentence
example1: Don't forget the full stop at the end.
example1_ru: Не забудь точку в конце.
example2: She added a full stop after the last word.

en_definition: a section of writing that deals with one main idea
example1: Start a new paragraph for each new idea.
example1_ru: Начинай новый абзац для каждой новой идеи.
example2: The essay had five clear paragraphs.

en_definition: to write the first, unfinished version of an essay
example1: We were asked to draft an essay about our hometown.
example1_ru: Нас попросили написать черновик эссе о нашем родном городе.
example2: She drafted an essay in one evening.

en_definition: to correct and improve a piece of writing
example1: Edit your essay carefully before the final submission.
example1_ru: Тщательно отредактируй своё эссе перед финальной сдачей.
example2: He edited the report to remove repeated ideas.

en_definition: a document used to collect opinions or comments
example1: Fill out the feedback form after the workshop.
example1_ru: Заполни форму обратной связи после воркшопа.
example2: The teacher read every feedback form carefully.

en_definition: a short course where people learn by doing practical activities
example1: We joined a writing workshop last weekend.
example1_ru: Мы посетили воркшоп по письму в прошлые выходные.
example2: The workshop taught useful presentation skills.

en_definition: a visitor invited to give a talk on a topic
example1: A guest speaker talked about careers in engineering.
example1_ru: Приглашённый докладчик рассказал о карьере в инженерии.
example2: Students asked the guest speaker many questions.

en_definition: a period after a talk when the audience can ask questions
example1: There was a short Q and A after the lecture.
example1_ru: После лекции была короткая сессия вопросов и ответов.
example2: The Q and A lasted about fifteen minutes.

en_definition: to mention a worry or problem to someone
example1: She raised a concern about the tight deadline.
example1_ru: Она выразила беспокойство насчёт сжатого срока.
example2: Students can raise a concern with the office directly.

en_definition: to introduce a subject into a conversation
example1: He brought up a topic no one had discussed before.
example1_ru: Он поднял тему, которую раньше никто не обсуждал.
example2: She brought up a topic related to housing costs.

en_definition: to start talking about something different
example1: He quickly changed the subject when asked about grades.
example1_ru: Он быстро сменил тему, когда его спросили об оценках.
example2: She tried to change the subject during the awkward moment.

en_definition: to have the same opinion as someone
example1: I completely agree with your idea about the project.
example1_ru: Я полностью согласен с твоей идеей насчёт проекта.
example2: She agreed with most of what the teacher said.

en_definition: to have a different opinion from someone
example1: I disagree with the plan to cancel the trip.
example1_ru: Я не согласен с планом отменить поездку.
example2: He politely disagreed with his classmate's answer.

en_definition: a particular way of thinking about a situation
example1: Everyone has a different point of view on this issue.
example1_ru: У каждого своя точка зрения на этот вопрос.
example2: Try to understand the other person's point of view.

en_definition: used to introduce your personal view on a topic
example1: In my opinion, the new schedule is much better.
example1_ru: По моему мнению, новое расписание намного лучше.
example2: In my opinion, the exam was fair.

en_definition: to choose something after thinking carefully
example1: We need to make a decision about housing by Friday.
example1_ru: Нам нужно принять решение насчёт жилья до пятницы.
example2: She made a decision to study abroad.

en_definition: to think carefully about the advantages and disadvantages of choices
example1: Take time to weigh the options before you choose.
example1_ru: Найди время взвесить варианты перед выбором.
example2: They weighed the options between two universities.

en_definition: the advantages and disadvantages of a decision
example1: Let's list the pros and cons of each option.
example1_ru: Давай перечислим плюсы и минусы каждого варианта.
example2: We discussed the pros and cons of living off campus.

en_definition: after everything has happened or been considered
example1: In the end, we chose the cheaper apartment.
example1_ru: В конце концов мы выбрали более дешёвую квартиру.
example2: In the end, everything worked out fine.

en_definition: at the earliest time that can be managed
example1: Please reply as soon as possible.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, ответьте как можно скорее.
example2: Send the form back as soon as possible.

en_definition: at the correct or expected time, not late
example1: The bus arrived exactly on time today.
example1_ru: Автобус прибыл сегодня точно вовремя.
example2: Try to submit your assignments on time.

en_definition: before something happens or is needed
example1: Book your train ticket in advance to save money.
example1_ru: Забронируй билет на поезд заранее, чтобы сэкономить деньги.
example2: Please pay the deposit in advance.

en_definition: very close to a deadline, without much preparation time
example1: He finished the essay at the last minute.
example1_ru: Он закончил эссе в последнюю минуту.
example2: We changed our plans at the last minute.

en_definition: occasionally, not very often
example1: We eat out from time to time as a treat.
example1_ru: Мы время от времени едим вне дома в качестве угощения.
example2: She calls her family from time to time.

en_definition: happening often and in a consistent pattern
example1: We meet on a regular basis to discuss the project.
example1_ru: Мы встречаемся на регулярной основе, чтобы обсудить проект.
example2: She exercises on a regular basis every week.

en_definition: for a period of time that is not very short or very long
example1: We chatted for a while before the class started.
example1_ru: Мы немного поболтали, прежде чем началось занятие.
example2: He lived in the dorm for a while before moving out.

en_definition: at the present time, as compared to the past
example1: Nowadays, most students use apps to study vocabulary.
example1_ru: В наши дни большинство студентов используют приложения для изучения слов.
example2: Nowadays, online classes are very common.

en_definition: describes something that happened regularly in the past but not now
example1: I used to walk to school every day.
example1_ru: Раньше я каждый день ходил в школу пешком.
example2: She used to live in a small village.

en_definition: almost never; very rarely
example1: He hardly ever misses a class.
example1_ru: Он почти никогда не пропускает занятия.
example2: I hardly ever eat fast food anymore.

en_definition: approximately; almost exactly
example1: The trip took more or less three hours.
example1_ru: Поездка заняла более или менее три часа.
example2: We finished the project more or less on time.

en_definition: used to say that your information might not be complete
example1: As far as I know, the exam is next Tuesday.
example1_ru: Насколько мне известно, экзамен в следующий вторник.
example2: As far as I know, the office is still open.

en_definition: used to introduce a frank or truthful opinion
example1: To be honest, I found the lecture confusing.
example1_ru: Если честно, лекция показалась мне запутанной.
example2: To be honest, I forgot about the deadline.

en_definition: used to introduce a new, related topic in conversation
example1: By the way, did you finish the assignment?
example1_ru: Кстати, ты закончил задание?
example2: By the way, the library closes early today.

en_definition: used to emphasize or add information to a statement
example1: As a matter of fact, I already sent the email.
example1_ru: На самом деле, я уже отправил письмо.
example2: As a matter of fact, she was right all along.

en_definition: used to explain something differently or more simply
example1: In other words, the deadline cannot be changed.
example1_ru: Другими словами, срок сдачи изменить нельзя.
example2: In other words, we need more time.

en_definition: used to introduce a final point that is still important
example1: Last but not least, remember to bring your passport.
example1_ru: И наконец, не забудь взять паспорт.
example2: Last but not least, thank you all for coming.

en_definition: used to introduce a contrasting point
example1: On the other hand, the cheaper flat is far from campus.
example1_ru: С другой стороны, более дешёвая квартира далеко от кампуса.
example2: On the other hand, we saved a lot of money.

en_definition: considering everything; overall
example1: All in all, the semester went really well.
example1_ru: В целом семестр прошёл действительно хорошо.
example2: All in all, I'm happy with my exam results.

en_definition: used to express disappointment about something
example1: What a pity you missed the concert!
example1_ru: Как жаль, что ты пропустил концерт!
example2: What a pity, the tickets were already sold out.
